programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When two couples vanish into thin air in the village of Monks Barton, Barnaby and Jones hear rumours of haunted woods and witchcraft. Barnaby remains sceptical of this, even when an unidentified body is found in a shallow grave and the missing start turning up dead. He soon has to determine if ghosts, criminals, or something else, is behind the abductions and murders.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy tackle two separate cases as Troy prepares to leave Midsomer after his promotion to DI.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's new Sergeant arrives on the day after the murder of a local shrew occurs, and he is immediately put to work.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Long-suppressed family secrets, stolen ancient artefacts, and a thirty year old death at a Celtic burial site result in a series of murders in present day Midsomer.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Midsomer hosts its 12th Annual Literary Festival, an author is found with his neck broken. More writers will die before the winner of the competition is announced.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby and DS Dan Scott investigate the shotgun murder of Jamie Cruikshank, a barman at the Maid in Splendour, a pub in the village of Midsomer Worthy.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When the curate of the local church in Midsomer Parva is burned alive in the effigy of the straw woman, DCI Tom Barnaby and DS Dan Scott find themselves investigating what must be a murder.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Exactly nine years after Ferdy Villers killed himself, his entire family reunites for Christmas unaware that someone is out for revenge.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a survivalist is found murdered in his bombproof shelter, Barnaby unravels a dark conspiracy to reveal how far people will go to escape the end of the world.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gated estate Challis Court is a tight-knit community for retired police officers, but when a new arrival is found dead, the other residents regard the murder as an affront. Barnaby realizes that the killer is likely an ex-police officer.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Pastry chef Chrissie Larkton's mill has been in her family for centuries. She restored it and turned it into a thriving online business, but the popularity has overshadowed Lower Blissingham, and many of the villagers are not happy about it.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Elverton-Cum-Latterly battle in the Dominoes Competition that dates back decades, but when the event occurs the same night as a drag queen fundraiser for a terminally ill teenager, it results in a deadly clash of cultures.

programme.title: Super Sleuths - Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Murders team opens up about their experience of working on the popular crime drama and provides insight into the overall process of bringing it to the small screen.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new Forensic Pathologist Kam Karimore are drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching as they seek to identify the villain.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Mysterious lights are seen in the sky over famous UFO hot-spot Cooper Hill. When a local forest-ranger suffers a strange death the visiting UFO spotters are convinced aliens are responsible. DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets in their search for the truth.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle. When the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson find themselves drawn into a ruthlessly competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed where winning comes at any cost.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the picturesque Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new Sculpture Park. But when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson have to get creative to crack a case where art imitates death, and everything has a deeper meaning.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a Saint's bones at an archaeological dig causes a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. But when the leader of the dig is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson soon realise that it's not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death come to Midsomer village Whitcombe Mallet. When the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson have to unravel a complex feud from the past, where nothing is what it seems...

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Peter and Caroline Cave are house-hunting in Midsomer Newton. Shortly before leaving their hotel they mention they haven't been able to find what they're looking for; "something old and derelict that they can do up." Anne Merrick tells them about Winyard, a tumbledown cottage in the woods, might be for sale. At the estate agents they are told the house is not yet available for viewing. Later that evening they find the house on their own. However, Caroline has read up on the cottage's haunted reputation and only reluctantly explores it with Peter. The next morning they are found strangled in their own car. DCI Barnaby starts the investigation with the help of PC Ben Jones and they come across the world of estate agents, identical twins, ghosts, piano wire, and more before another body turns up.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Villagers of Midsomer Barton are all set to celebrate Oak Apple Week, especially as the carnival queen event is set to return after an eight year absence. Yet the body of the mother of a deceased, one-time carnival queen, found in a stream, looks set to spoil things, as Barnaby and Jones find themselves digging up the past. From familiar faces, to drunken boasts and hidden obessions, it slowly becomes clear that the mother's daughter might be the key to solving the murder, when more deaths follow.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Wealthy, eccentric and obese aristocrat Freddy Butler drops dead, shortly after gathering all three of his wives at Haddington Hall to make an announcement. Although George Bullard is adamant his death is from natural causes, the case is far from simple when Freddy's solicitor is deliberately killed in a house fire shortly afterwards and Freddy's will goes missing. Barnaby and Jones find themselves investigating the extended Butler family, uncovering hidden secrets, illegitimate children and elusive treasure in the process.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Martin Barrett, a local council clerk living in Midsomer Worthy, is murdered during the night by shotgun, prompting Barnaby and Jones to find a motive for his murder. When they discover that Barrett was an expert blackmailer, his victims become prime suspects for his death - from a local pub landlord, a cleaner, a prominent member of the police board, to a former marine geologist. As they investigate, finding themselves drawn to the seaside, they slowly uncover deception, theft, attempted blackmail and hatred.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: In the village of Broughton, a ninety-year-old battle of the sexes has often occurred, with men trying to oppose an event that the women have managed to successfully hold without interruption, until it culminates with casualties on both sides - Mildred Danvers, an elderly woman who had returned on the day of a funeral and poisoned in her hotel room, and Rev. Anthony Gant, shot during a major event being held in the village green, in view of many. It soon becomes clear someone may have wished to kill both to cover up a dark truth, and soon Barnaby has to figure out who amongst the village wanted both dead, complicated further when Gant's curate is murdered next.

programme.desc: An extremely agitated woman gets placed in a restraint chair; a young woman with a drug addiction is booked on prostitution charges; an uncooperative and drunken man refuses to change into his jail uniform.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: After being arrested for domestic violence, a man is placed in restraints; an intoxicated man picks a fight with inmates over their hometown; a woman is arrested for assaulting theater guards.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Officers have difficulty booking an incomprehensible man; a commotion at a church ends in arrest; a man wearing a tuxedo is detained after mocking officers for wearing "pig suits."

programme.desc: A woman lies hobbled in the backseat of a patrol car after kicking the officer in the face; officers struggle to control an outrageous man; officers attend to a woman struck by a drunk driver.

programme.desc: A hysterical woman is transferred to an isolation cell; a woman shows her undying LA pride to the arresting officers; a young woman is put under observation for threatening harm to herself.

programme.desc: A testy man is sent to the isolation cell to "cool off"; teary-eyed young man maintains his innocence; an inmate may have ingested contraband to avoid possession charges.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Taye Diggs has been a lifelong actor and performer. He made his Broadway debut in 1994 and became a Black film heartthrob after starring in How Stella Got Her Groove Back.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Actress and director Salli Richardson-Whitfield discusses starring in classic films and taking her talents behind the camera to direct some of television�s most popular shows.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Will Downing is the Prince of Sophisticated Soul, with a 30-year career that includes chart topping singles in Dance, R&B, and Jazz. But at the height of his career, a paralyzing bout with auto-immune disease threatened his life.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Teddy Pendergrass,one of the most popular black recording artist in the world, and the first artist to achieve five consecutive multi-platinum albums, was an icon of romantic balladry.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: When Angela Winbush came on the scene in the 1980s, she brought not only a powerful sultry voice infused with gospel roots, but also a versatile self-contained songwriting and producing talent at a time when few females, and even fewer African American females, were producing records at all.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: They were the Marvin Gaye and Tammi Terrell of their time � an unlikely pairing of opposite personalities that made musical magic together. Alexander O�Neal grew up poor and troubled in Mississippi, while Cherrelle was the pampered daughter of a successful attorney in Beverly Hills. He sang ballads with the gruff-voiced soul of a Teddy Pendergrass, while she showed off an appealing voice and good looks on infectiously fast paced dance hits. But both artists, whose most popular songs were often produced by the stellar team of Jimmy Jam and Terry Lewis, really took off when they were teamed together on unforgettable duets like �Saturday Love.� Then, poised on the brink of superstardom, both of their careers abruptly fell apart, a casualty of both self-destructive acts and unforeseen tragedies. On this remarkably candid episode of �Unsung�, Alexander O�Neal and Cherrelle tell the stories of two extraordinary characters whose legacies will forever be entwined.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: In an age of popular family singing groups, from the Jacksons to the Osmonds, the Sylvers were the largest and the liveliest of them all. Nine members strong, they became best known for their disco friendly hits �Boogie Fever� and �Hot Line�, which drew inevitable comparisons to the sound of the Jackson 5. But the Sylvers were performing together long before that. Even as young children, they made their mark on television as �The Little Angels�, and later scaled the charts with songs written by their oldest brother, Leon Sylvers III, from �Fool�s Paradise� to �Misdemeanor�, that showed off their musical range and uncanny family harmonies. But internal conflicts and fights with their management broke up the group at the height of their career, and in its aftermath some of their members fell prey to drink and drugs, with dire consequences. Yet through it all the Sylvers remain united as a family, finding strength in their trials, as they reveal their full story in this poignant episode of �Unsung.�

programme.title: For The Love of Ruth
programme.desc: A young woman meets a God-fearing matriarch and a man who loves her unconditionally.

programme.title: Chasing Waterfalls
programme.desc: A godly woman rises to the top of the fashion world before she is brought down by her greed and quest for power.

programme.title: Runaway Island
programme.desc: A diverse group of guests at a tropical island resort find their lives intersecting when a hurricane strikes, forcing them to bond as they seek refuge.

programme.title: Bad Dad Rehab
programme.desc: Four men learn how to truly become dads. One would rather pay child support than actually spend time with his kids, and another one spoils himself with new shoes while skimping on his son's winter coat.

programme.title: Bad Dad Rehab: The Next Session
programme.desc: BAD DAD REHAB: THE NEXT SESSION follows the new class of Bad Dads hailing from Jersey City. These men must confront their personal demons in order to get their act together and become better dads.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Donald Lawrence has left a legacy as one of the most important figures in contemporary gospel music. But success has not come without its share of struggles, from criticism for infusing secular music into faith-based music to his battle with cancer.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Hezekiah Walker is a singer and pastor whose ministry has pushed the boundaries of traditional church and worship. Bishop Walker gets candid about the ups and downs of a trailblazing career and his personal journey that inspired his greatest hits.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Thelma Houston has been performing and entertaining fans for over 50 years with her soulful and powerful voice. In this episode of �Unsung�, Thelma, along with friends and family, come together to tell her incredible journey.

On this remarkably candid episode of �Unsung�, Alexander O�Neal and Cherrelle tell the stories of two extraordinary characters whose legacies will forever be entwined.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: In an age of popular family singing groups, from the Jacksons to the Osmonds, the Sylvers were the largest and the liveliest of them all. Nine members strong, they became best known for their disco friendly hits �Boogie Fever� and �Hot Line�, which drew inevitable comparisons to the sound of the Jackson 5. But the Sylvers were performing together long before that. Even as young children, they made their mark on television as �The Little Angels�, and later scaled the charts with songs written by their oldest brother, Leon Sylvers III, from �Fool�s Paradise� to �Misdemeanor�, that showed off their musical range and uncanny family harmonies. But internal conflicts and fights with their management broke up the group at the height of their career, and in its aftermath some of their members fell prey to drink and drugs, with dire consequences. Yet through it all the Sylvers remain united as a family, finding strength in their trials, as they reveal their full story in this poignant episode of �Unsung.�

programme.title: Bad Dad Rehab
programme.desc: Four men learn how to truly become dads. One would rather pay child support than actually spend time with his kids, and another one spoils himself with new shoes while skimping on his son's winter coat.

programme.title: Bad Dad Rehab: The Next Session
programme.desc: BAD DAD REHAB: THE NEXT SESSION follows the new class of Bad Dads hailing from Jersey City. These men must confront their personal demons in order to get their act together and become better dads.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Teddy Pendergrass,one of the most popular black recording artist in the world, and the first artist to achieve five consecutive multi-platinum albums, was an icon of romantic balladry.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: When Angela Winbush came on the scene in the 1980s, she brought not only a powerful sultry voice infused with gospel roots, but also a versatile self-contained songwriting and producing talent at a time when few females, and even fewer African American females, were producing records at all.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: They were the Marvin Gaye and Tammi Terrell of their time � an unlikely pairing of opposite personalities that made musical magic together. Alexander O�Neal grew up poor and troubled in Mississippi, while Cherrelle was the pampered daughter of a successful attorney in Beverly Hills. He sang ballads with the gruff-voiced soul of a Teddy Pendergrass, while she showed off an appealing voice and good looks on infectiously fast paced dance hits. But both artists, whose most popular songs were often produced by the stellar team of Jimmy Jam and Terry Lewis, really took off when they were teamed together on unforgettable duets like �Saturday Love.� Then, poised on the brink of superstardom, both of their careers abruptly fell apart, a casualty of both self-destructive acts and unforeseen tragedies. On this remarkably candid episode of �Unsung�, Alexander O�Neal and Cherrelle tell the stories of two extraordinary characters whose legacies will forever be entwined.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: For five decades and counting Betty Wright has brought the “real” to R&B, with a powerful voice and sassy delivery that produced over ten top twenty hits. But those songs reflected aspects of a harder life, as label troubles and family tragedy threatened to silence her voice.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Donald Lawrence has left a legacy as one of the most important figures in contemporary gospel music. But success has not come without its share of struggles, from criticism for infusing secular music into faith-based music to his battle with cancer.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Hezekiah Walker is a singer and pastor whose ministry has pushed the boundaries of traditional church and worship. Bishop Walker gets candid about the ups and downs of a trailblazing career and his personal journey that inspired his greatest hits.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Thelma Houston has been performing and entertaining fans for over 50 years with her soulful and powerful voice. In this episode of �Unsung�, Thelma, along with friends and family, come together to tell her incredible journey.

programme.title: To Hell And Back
programme.desc: In a modern retelling of the story of Job, a devout businessman falls out of favor with God.

programme.desc: Flourishing in their careers, Madison and Alec begin to explore their romance. However, reality strikes as the two face new distractions and obstacles that threaten to derail the fate of their relationship.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Uncle Si has a brush with death, he creates a bucket list and asks the guys to help him fulfill his dreams. Meanwhile, Kay sets out to prove to Willie and Alan that she actually graduated high school.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Duck Commander sponsors a NASCAR race, Jase and the guys get themselves in the mood by attempting to work as a pit crew. Meanwhile, Willie and Korie challenge John Luke and his fianc Mary Kate to a doubles tennis match.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: In this very special wedding episode, John Luke Robertson is getting married to his fiancé Mary Kate, and our cameras are with him every step of the way.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Missy's parents visit, her father convinces Jase to take him on a quail hunt. But while their "bonding hunt" yields very few quail, it does bring them closer together as friends.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Si recruits his nephews to help him transport a storage container to his backyard, but things take a turn when Jep becomes locked inside the box.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Willie's old rival Phillip McMillan challenges him to a game of dodgeball, Willie recruits his family members to settle a score once and for all.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Jase and Phil make a bet about whether or not human beings in a duck blind are visible from the air, Jep buys a drone copter to get a "bird's eye" view, but ends up losing it in the process.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase and the guys convince Willie to hire his older brother Alan to work at Duck Commander, but when Alan begins micromanaging everyone, they quickly change their mind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: When the Robertsons throw a Renaissance Fair birthday party for Godwin, various family members compete in a tournament to win knighthood.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase accompanies Willie on a business trip to Arkansas to pitch a new product and has unorthodox sales strategies. Back in Monroe, Jep and Si have to track down an important item of Willie's that they accidentally shipped out.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie thinks his kids have been spending too much time on their phones and other devices so he initiates a "No-Tech Week," but when he has to participate too it ends up being harder than he thought.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: It's day 53 of the ice road season and only the toughest truckers remain on a road that's sent many people packing.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Spring brings a rush of warm weather and the big melt begins. The road is nearly empty as only the toughest or craziest truckers remain.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: It is a job only a few would dare. This year, four ice road truckers, Hugh, Alex, Rick and Drew, return from last season to join Arctic veterans.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: After just two days in the Arctic, the critical demands of the oil exploration sites are testing the limits of the truckers and their machines.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Just five days into the Arctic adventure, Mother Nature is about to strike and wreak havoc on the ice roads.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: It's just over a month into the new ice road season and more than 800 truckers are on the ice, working around the clock.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: It's day 37 of the ice road season and there almost 3,000 more loads to go. Drivers are washing out and the road is beaten up.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: It's week two on the frozen tundra and the name of the game is Rig Move. MGM is breaking down camp and moving every last piece of equipment to a new base at Langley.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: After the storm clears, most of the truckers are ready to roll--all except Rick, who is on the verge of quitting and looks to Hugh for advice.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: The truckers have delivered the fragile 135-foot derrick to the new MGM exploration site.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Bear and Eric must haul a boiler and a flare stack--two critical pieces of equipment.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: After being Med-Evaced to Yellowknife hospital, Alex receives a grim diagnosis that could mean the end of his Ice Road season.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Research site before the ice road melts, but while scientists are on the verge of an historic breakthrough, the truckers are starting to haul away equipment piece-by-piece.

programme.desc: With the sun beating down on the ice, the road is melting at an accelerating rate.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: There's no rest for the righteous and nobody knows this better then the Dog. He is committed to saving his new friend's bail bonding business, and now must race against time to bring a rogues gallery to justice.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Can Dog complete his mission to clean-up this frontier town? With four outlaws still on the lam, Dog sets out to bring 'em in. As day turns into night and with the darkness and the danger mounting, an undaunted Dog and team plan late night hits.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the posse travel to the Big Island to help Leland track down a small-time Bonnie and Clyde couple. Their crime: theft, including allegedly stealing a pair of Buddha statues for resale. But this only masks deeper problems with the couple.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: This fugitive is hard to pin down in Honolulu's downtown district--moving from hostel to hotel to apartment high-rise. But as the hunt evolves, Dog discovers that this alleged burglar isn't the only family member wanted by the law.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's relentless ruthlessness is being put to the test on the search for the elusive fugitive, Marco. Dog is tracking him across Colorado, from Grand Junction to Denver.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: This fugitive has a bad reputation and a knack for running from the law. The pursuit has Dog and team traveling through an urban dystopia of suspicion, false leads and mistrust.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and Beth take time from their busy schedule to thank their fans by inviting them into their home to celebrate the holiday season. But when Dog decides he needs to help his son Garry believe in Santa Claus again the plan changes.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Things are seldom what they seem in the world of bounty hunting and it is through this maze of illusions that Dog pursues his prey.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is back in Colorado chasing a young man with a taste for trouble. He's fled to Texas but with his family's house on the line Dog is willing to try a risky strategy. He enlists the family in an elaborate hoax to lure the runner home.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog knows that crime and drugs not only damage victims but also harm the families of the perpetrators, especially the kids. And both of the fugitives he's chasing today have left shattered families and struggling women in their wake.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog just can't resist a challenge, especially if it's to help out a friend. Jim is a bondsman and longtime friend of Dog. Jim's son has just started in the family business.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Darkness has fallen, but with the trail hot and the stakes high Dog and posse refuse to let up. The "Big Fish" in this case is Marco, an alleged drug dealer with a big bond.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: This fugitive has a bad reputation and a knack for running from the law. The pursuit has Dog and team traveling through an urban dystopia of suspicion, false leads and mistrust.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Beth buys Dog a shirt with an embroidered chicken that looks like a dragon, but Dog explains he only wears shirts with pictures of either Jesus or a Harley-Davidson. When it's time to go to work, the team goes after Necia.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is sick as a dog, but he still must lead the hunt for a fugitive named James. James' girlfriend tries to cover for him, but Dog surprises James in his bedroom and leads him away in cuffs.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Bounty hunts are always full of surprises and that is very true today. Dog is in search of a potentially dangerous fugitive, he's only 19 and charged with kidnapping and menacing.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: It hurts Dog to see people who after years of walking the straight and narrow, suddenly lose their way. But today's case is a member of the law enforcement community who has recently gone astray, and that truly troubles the Dog.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's target for today has been in trouble with the law since he was a juvenile. And he seems willing to use any trick in the system to avoid capture by Dog. In this match of wits, will Dog prevail?

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Being hunted by the Dog is a lousy way to celebrate your birthday but that's exactly the spot that Robert finds himself in today. He's missed his court date, quit his job, and vacated his last known address.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is back in Denver and in hot pursuit of a woman wanted for identity theft. The sad reality is that the identify which has been robbed is her own. She's traded in a bright future for a life on the wrong side of the law.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: It's Dog's birthday, and he plans to celebrate in the same way he has for the last twenty years. There's no taking the day off to indulge himself for this bounty hunter--he's tracking down a fugitive!

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog told him not to go, but Charles went anyway, skipping to Oregon against the terms of his bail. Now Dog has a tip that Charles plans to slip back into Honolulu. With $30,000 on the line Dog knows he can leave little to chance.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Going after two fugitives in one day is tough enough without the facing Colorado's notoriously unpredictable weather. Today it's the threat of sever thunderstorm, hail and area wide tornado warnings.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog believes in safety first, and today's hunt puts this philosophy to the test. The fugitive's problem with anger management increases the dangers of their already risky line of work. Dog warns the crew to be ready for anything as they head out.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the team are back in Colorado hunting bounties. Today's fugitive has a long rap sheet and a reputation for packing heat. With nothing but an address and phone number, the team heads out on the hunt.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: "When you don't write the bond, you don't know the prey," as Dog always says. So when Dog and team help out a bondsman friend whose client jumped bail, the target is a blank slate. Are his tattoos gang related? Is he likely to be violent?

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: With twelve kids of his own Dog knows how hard it is to keep children on the straight and narrow, especially during their turbulent teens.

programme.desc: (4/4) The obsessed Pangol is determined to give Argolis a future and strike back at the Foamasi.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Sontaran Experiment

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Sontaran Experiment
programme.desc: (2/2) Sarah has been captured by Styre who is conducting experiments on the captured humans.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Destiny of the Daleks
programme.desc: (1/4) The TARDIS lands on the old Dalek homeworld of Skaro.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Destiny of the Daleks
programme.desc: (2/4) Romana is captured and interrogated by the Daleks whilst the Doctor meets the Movellans.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Destiny of the Daleks
programme.desc: (3/4) Davros has awoken from his centuries long slumber and is taken prisoner by the Doctor.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Destiny of the Daleks
programme.desc: (4/4) The Doctor discovers that the Daleks need Davros to break the stalemate in their war with the Movellans.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Hand of Fear
programme.desc: (2/4) The hand builds power and reacts to nuclear radiation, causing an overload situation.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Hand of Fear
programme.desc: (3/4) The fossilised hand has regenerated into a Kastrian called Eldrad who appears to be female

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Revenge of the Cybermen
programme.desc: (1/4) The Doctor, Sarah and Harry return to Nerva, but at an earlier point in time.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Hand of Fear
programme.desc: (4/4) Eldrad reconfigures his body to its male form and states he will return to Earth to rule.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Revenge of the Cybermen
programme.desc: (2/4) Sarah is the latest victim of the space plague.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Revenge of the Cybermen

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Revenge of the Cybermen

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Creature from the Pit

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Creature from the Pit

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: The Androids of Tara
programme.desc: (4/4) The Doctor is determined to prevent Grendal from ruling over the planet Tara.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: State of Decay
programme.desc: (3/4) The Doctor discovers that he is facing an unstoppable foe from Time Lord history.

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: State of Decay
programme.desc: (4/4) The Great Vampire begins to awaken from his long sleep...

programme.title: Classic Doctor Who: Full Circle
programme.desc: (3/4) What is the secret of the Starliner. Why is chaos closing in on the Deciders?

programme.desc: (4/4) What is the legacy of Dexter's research? How does the Doctor give evolution a small nudge?

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Over 10,000 crop circles, appearing in 50 different countries, have been reported; examining whether they are all part of an elaborate hoax or not.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In Australia, Aboriginal people tell sacred stories about sky gods visiting Earth in fire-spewing crafts and "seeding" the planet.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Spanish Conquistadors obliterated the historical record of the Maya in the 16th century, but did they wipe out evidence that ancient people traveled to the Americas from all over the world, and in flying craft provided by extraterrestrial visitors.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos and David Childress explore the mysterious chain of manmade islets in Micronesia's Nan Madol; built from 250 million tons of volcanic rock, there are local legends that strange visitors floated the massive rocks through the air.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Satellite images have revealed a giant face that appears to be carved into the Earth in Alberta, Canada, which has now become known far and wide as "The Badlands Guardian."

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Mythological tales of intimate encounters with otherworldly beings point to possible alien intervention contributing to the genetic makeup of humans.

programme.desc: The ancient and modern-day superheroes may have originated from the same legends, which could have otherworldy origins.

programme.desc: Giorgio A. Tsoukalos, David Childress and William Henry examine a recently discovered lost city in Peru that has upended the historical timeline of South America, revealing that civilization emerged thousands of years earlier than previously thought.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Examining the unique geology and location of Mount Shasta in northern California, to determine why it is known as a hotbed of strange events, from Big Foot sightings to UFO encounters to hikers vanishing without a trace.

programme.desc: A countdown of the world's top 10 alien cover-ups, from the famous Roswell Incident to a secret military base rumored to be housing ET technology to recently released UFO videos captured by cameras on Navy fighter jets.

programme.desc: Counting down the world's top 10 alien encounters, from the first reported abduction event to mass UFO sightings over New York's Hudson Valley to an incident in ancient Rome.

programme.desc: A multitude of guests and scientists explores everything from human origins to groundbreaking archaeological finds, new understandings of the cosmos, and the profound possibility that humans are not alone in the universe.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A family's worst nightmare occurs when 16-year-old Becky Watts goes missing, and is found dead days later, murdered by her own stepbrother.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When police in a small Washington State community began looking into the disappearance of a 22-year-old woman, they had no way of knowing it would evolve into a nationwide hunt for a sexual sadist. Police knew the killer's identity, but he was a master of disguise. The FBI was called on to help. Agents discovered that several unsuspecting women across the northwest had fallen prey to his charms.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In November of 1994, when a Wells Fargo armored van disappeared from a Glendale, Arizona mall, local authorities and the FBI scrambled to recover it, and its cargo of nearly a million dollars. Six hours later, agents found the van, its driver shot dead inside and the money gone. It was a perfect hit by professionals who left no clue behind. Months passed before a solid lead developed. In January, a tip led agents to look into a former prison guard.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 90s, Washington, D.C. police were losing the battle against street gangs who enforced their territories with lethal violence. The homicide rate in the nation's capital climbed to the highest in the country. Local police turned to the FBI for help, but agents soon found themselves in the direct line of fire. In the fall of 1994, a gang member walked into police headquarters, killed two FBI agents and one detective, then turned the gun on himself.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: From May until July of 1984, Alton Coleman and his common law wife, Denise Brown, engaged in a gruesome crime spree that spanned six states and left eight people dead including children. The FBI coordinated law enforcement efforts throughout the entire country in order to track the couple. Their expertise in conducting manhunts for the nation's most horrible killers paid off. The couple was located and apprehended as they prepared to kill again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: At first it seemed like a tragic hunting accident when 18-year-old David Wilkey was shot to death in rural Virginia in 1983. A tenacious insurance investigator suspected foul play, but he had no direct evidence. Two years later a man contacted North Carolina FBI agents with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire: he claimed he had been hired to murder Wilkey's killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A 1981 armored car robbery near New York City left one guard dead and another critically wounded. Police stopped suspects nearby and another shoot-out ensued, claiming the lives of two officers. Local police and the FBI suspected a large group of domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. They would try to ferret out these heavily armed criminals who moved in a shadowy underworld and were not afraid to kill authorities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: By April of 1995 in Washington, D.C., two officers had been shot and one had been murdered. Each had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man sneaked up and opened fire. The FBI was brought in to help catch the cop killer. Agents identified the man who was most likely the killer, but he was nowhere to be found. Months of surveillance operations around the country finally yielded a break; they found their suspect in Baltimore, MD.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a successful television producer was gunned down in his New York office. It looked like a professional hit. With no leads, the investigation stalled. Four years later and a continent away, a man told Los Angeles police he had been hired to murder two exotic dancers. Investigators linked the two crimes and uncovered a businessman willing to kill to protect his interests.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Anchorage, Alaska, a young mother and her two daughters were sexually assaulted and brutally murdered. There was only one piece of forensic evidence: a washrag carrying pubic lice that the killer used to clean himself after the crime. Employing an unusual and original method, an FBI expert combed through all the hairs and fibers in the apartment. He was not only able to determine how long each hair had been there, but which hairs came from the killer and how the crime proceeded.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 80's, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation. Regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. Desperate to stop the violence, local authorities turned to the FBI for help. One dealer bowed to no one. He would stop at nothing to keep detectives and agents from building a case against him. When his organization began targeting the government itself, a city went on high alert.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Racial tensions in Buffalo in 1980 were at an all-time high. Six black males had been brutally murdered--two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets. Residents screamed for the police to stop this serial killer. With no suspects, local investigators turned to the FBI. Profilers were able to produce a psychological composite of the type of person the killer would most likely be. Their analysis paid off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984 had the authorities puzzled and the community scared. Since the beginning, the FBI had been analyzing the crime scene evidence and had found a common link: red trilobal carpet fibers. But there was no suspect, until one of the killer's potential victims survived. Through her interviews and the cooperation of local authorities and the FBI, 31-year-old Bobby Joe Long emerged as the prime suspect.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980s, the decapitation of a young African-American male led the Miami-Dade police department and the FBI along a trail of murder. Investigators quickly discovered that the victim was a dissident member of a religious sect headed by a man calling himself Yahweh ben Yahweh, or God, Son of God. Although the FBI believed Yahweh ben Yahweh and his followers were responsible for many murders, they could find no direct evidence linking them to the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Local law enforcement became stumped when the body of an unidentified young woman turned up on a highway in their small Pennsylvania town. They turned to the FBI labs. The hi-tech lab enabled investigators and agents to identify the victim. Knowing who she was allowed agents to reconstruct her every move. Though the road to finding her killer was twisted and long, agents persisted.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a California man was found dismembered in 1990, police suspected a local burglar, but they couldn't find him. Six months later, he was caught robbing an Arizona bank and sentenced to life. But prison couldn't hold him for long, and within a year he escaped. The FBI would lead a multi-agency manhunt for a killer on a desperate run through the tourist regions of the west.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A serial killer's rampage was discovered when the bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves that dotted the Alaskan wilderness. The killer taunted his victims, hunting them like animals before shooting them down. He was at home in the rugged terrain. But the hunt for him began 3000 miles away, where FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a small crime free Indiana town was turned inside out by the arrival of one dangerous stranger. After an officer was gunned down at point-blank range, the psychotic killer led investigators across the Midwest. No one knew where the gunman would strike next, even as his tally of murders and abductions grew. The FBI and state police raced against time to rescue America's heartland from this deranged gunman, before he killed again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children. Police searched for answers, but found none. The horrible crime appeared unmotivated, senseless, and random. Then, family members offered a startling clue. The victim, the wife of a prominent Atlanta attorney, was also a woman burdened with a secret; a secret the FBI believed led to her death. It would force local and federal agents to untangle an intricate web of money, drugs and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a chilly afternoon in 1989, Marine Captain Shirley Russell disappeared from Quantico, Virginia. Captain Russell was a dedicated officer; her friends and colleagues were certain she had not gone AWOL. FBI agents found her husband, a former Marine, was acting strangely, and a blood-like stain on the couple's floor disappeared before agents could examine it. There was no body, no witness and no weapon.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Labor Day weekend turned into a nightmare of murder as a cross-country crime spree terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and a teenage boy disappeared within hours of each other. Police believed the cases were linked, but didn't know how. They contacted the FBI for help. A trail of stolen vehicles and murder victims across seven states led agents on a hunt for two suspected killers. Their only hope of stopping them was to anticipate the killers' next move.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1988, a wealthy Jackson, Mississippi woman disappeared from her home in broad daylight. Left behind was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names and no specific instructions. The note, and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate. FBI and local police struggled to find answers in the cryptic message so they could find the kidnapper and retrieve the victim before her time ran out. Solving the crime required the keen insight of a criminal profiler and the perseverance of a family, local authorities, and the FBI.

programme.title: Los archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El 21 de junio de 1964, tres defensores de los derechos civiles, uno blanco y uno negro, desaparecieron de Mississippi mientras cruzaban el condado de Neshoba. Una investigación del FBI a gran escala llevó a la recuperación de los cuerpos de los hombres asesinados y la evidencia física fue recuperada y analizada. Los laboratorios del FBI confirmaron que todas excepto una de las balas usadas para asesinar a los hombres provenían de la misma arma. El FBI se ganó la confianza de un informante, cuya información condujo a la condena de siete miembros del Ku Klux Klan local.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her infant son mysteriously disappeared from their home. The woman's husband was the only suspect, but he was eventually cleared. There was no solid evidence that a crime had even been committed. Six months later, a local farmer found the remains of the missing woman and her child. Both had been murdered. Agents and local police had only one tiny piece of evidence, an unidentified hair found in the murdered woman's car. The hair was too small for analysis.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a series of brutal bank robberies and murders in south Miami during the mid-1980's, the FBI embarked on an exhaustive eight-month investigation. With little evidence and no suspects, agents were willing to try anything to find the violent perpetrators. On April 11, 1986, agents conducting routine surveillance spotted the vehicle used in the bank robberies. They attempted to arrest the two suspects in the car. What followed was the bloodiest firefight in FBI history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a young boy and his mother were kidnapped and murdered by a vicious drug cartel, FBI agents in the field worked tirelessly to pierce the secretive drug underworld and unearth those involved. Their surveillance and investigative work uncovered a violent trail of murder and greed. FBI agents and forensic examiners pieced together the evidence needed to draw out and then crush the cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, a series of crimes in a small Iowa farming community left residents reeling in shock and fear. Two young men went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder, and then disappeared. By the time they were identified, they already had a twelve-hour lead. As the hunt for the two men expanded, the FBI committed its resources to tracking down the suspects. The pursuit would take agents across six states to catch the killers before they struck again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In February of 1996, New Jersey millionaire Frank Black flew to Florida to close a major business deal before retiring. He never came home. Authorities in New Jersey sensed foul play and brought in the FBI. Agents learned of a mystery woman in Florida who was to meet Black. Phone records showed a flood of calls to Black from his business rival just before the disappearance.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her infant son mysteriously disappeared from their home. The woman's husband was the only suspect, but he was eventually cleared. There was no solid evidence that a crime had even been committed. Six months later, a local farmer found the remains of the missing woman and her child. Both had been murdered. Agents and local police had only one tiny piece of evidence, an unidentified hair found in the murdered woman's car. The hair was too small for analysis.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A day that began with a class field trip ended with a parent's worst nightmare. An 11-year-old girl was missing. Her parents were certain she was no runaway. When they failed to find her in their quiet Illinois neighborhood, they called the police. The FBI soon joined the investigation and family and friends worked with detectives and agents in an urgent search for the missing girl. Faced with conflicting witness reports and false leads, agents wouldn't stop until they found the girl, and the truth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A 1981 armored car robbery near New York City left one guard dead and another critically wounded. Police stopped suspects nearby and another shoot-out ensued, claiming the lives of two officers. Local police and the FBI suspected a large group of domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. They would try to ferret out these heavily armed criminals who moved in a shadowy underworld and were not afraid to kill authorities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1988, a wealthy Jackson, Mississippi woman disappeared from her home in broad daylight. Left behind was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names and no specific instructions. The note, and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate. FBI and local police struggled to find answers in the cryptic message so they could find the kidnapper and retrieve the victim before her time ran out. Solving the crime required the keen insight of a criminal profiler and the perseverance of a family, local authorities, and the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Alabama, a pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country. Eluding authorities, they moved from state to state in a wave of abduction and car theft. When a foreign military officer disappeared along their trail, police and FBI agents wondered if the armed fugitives had become killers. Agents believed the pair were growing more violent and raced to find the escapees before more victims crossed their path.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a gunman fired into rush hour traffic. The brazen daytime shooting of employees from the Central Intelligence Agency stunned the country. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political. The FBI was called in, but the nationwide search quickly became a far-reaching international manhunt. Agents processed more than 2000 leads and crossed an ocean in the effort to deliver justice.

programme.title: Los archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El 21 de junio de 1964, tres defensores de los derechos civiles, uno blanco y uno negro, desaparecieron de Mississippi mientras cruzaban el condado de Neshoba. Una investigación del FBI a gran escala llevó a la recuperación de los cuerpos de los hombres asesinados y la evidencia física fue recuperada y analizada. Los laboratorios del FBI confirmaron que todas excepto una de las balas usadas para asesinar a los hombres provenían de la misma arma. El FBI se ganó la confianza de un informante, cuya información condujo a la condena de siete miembros del Ku Klux Klan local.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Claude Dallas lived the life of a cowboy. A loner and distrustful of authority, he lived off the land, poaching wildlife and selling the hides. But when two game wardens confronted Dallas about his deeds, they were shot and killed. After a massive manhunt, Dallas was captured and convicted, only to escape months later. Making the FBI's Ten Most Wanted list, Dallas eluded the law for almost a year until the FBI tracked him down in a small town in California.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. Disguises and speed protected their identity from cameras and the police as they stole hundreds of thousands of dollars, always leaving a bomb to terrorize their victims. The stakes were raised when the FBI discovered the robbers had an agenda beyond their personal wealth. To stop these extremists and their growing operation, the FBI needed to capture their leaders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Memphis, Tennessee, a horrible crime terrified local residents. When a wealthy young mother was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The twenty-five year old victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband, and several spurned boyfriends. All were on the FBI's short list. To find the woman and her abductor, investigators would have to first determine the motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, a triple murder terrified residents in the nation's capital. In a Georgetown coffee shop, three employees were shot to death. Investigators could find no immediate suspects and no clear motive for the attack. Evidence at the crime scene pointed in many different directions. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong, or an act of cold-blooded vengeance against one of the victims?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On February 3, 1990, a 14-year-old girl ran away from her mother's home and disappeared without a trace. Her mother hoped Pasadena, Texas police could find her before it was too late. Six months later, their worst fear was realized when a farmer discovered the young girl's remains in an Illinois barn. The FBI was called in to help. Agents tracked down a trucker who they believed was responsible for 50 more deaths. Investigators knew they had to stop this menace of the highways.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a successful television producer was gunned down in his New York office. It looked like a professional hit. With no leads, the investigation stalled. Four years later and a continent away, a man told Los Angeles police he had been hired to murder two exotic dancers. Investigators linked the two crimes and uncovered a businessman willing to kill to protect his interests.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Residents of the small town of Noel, Missouri were shocked when one of their banks was robbed. When the bank president was nowhere to be found, the residents were outraged. The FBI discovered that things are not always as they appear. Minute clues led the agents to the body of the bank president. With no suspects, the FBI relied on dogged determination and modern forensics. Agents slowly pieced together tiny shreds of evidence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In November of 1994, when a Wells Fargo armored van disappeared from a Glendale, Arizona mall, local authorities and the FBI scrambled to recover it, and its cargo of nearly a million dollars. Six hours later, agents found the van, its driver shot dead inside and the money gone. It was a perfect hit by professionals who left no clue behind. Months passed before a solid lead developed. In January, a tip led agents to look into a former prison guard.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two couples found themselves on the same deserted island paradise. But only one couple left alive. Buck Walker and Stephanie Stearns arrived in Hawaii on the Sea Wind, which belonged to the missing couple, Mac and Muff Graham. Buck and Stephanie were arrested and served time for boat theft. But years later, bones were discovered on the deserted island and identified as the remains of Muff Graham.

programme.desc: Wedded couples vow to stay together, until death do they part. This series looks at what happens when a bride or groom causes their beloved’s demise.

programme.desc: When high school student Megan Nichols vanishes from her Illinois home, her mother finds a note saying that she ran away; a grisly discovery made three years later changes the course of the investigation.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When 16-year-old Britney Ujlaky disappears, a small town in Nevada bands together to track down a potential suspect - an unknown cowboy; Soon, their focus turns to someone much closer to Britney.

programme.title: Dateline NBC

programme.title: Dateline NBC

programme.title: Dateline NBC

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Alicia inicia sesiones con Valentina y descubre señales de abuso. Rafael le pide a Alicia que lo ayude a sacar a Manuel del caso de Valentina.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: Josué lidera a los hebreos rumbo a Canaán. Enfrenta diversos desafíos, como la oposición de Samara, quien está enamorada de él.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: Adela confiesa que Herodías es la responsable de que Salomé huyera del palacio. Jesús le demuestra a Pedro y a sus discípulos que él es el verdadero Hijo de Dios. Jesús pone a prueba la fe de Pedro para saber si es digno de ser uno de sus seguidores.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: Salomé le propone a Longino huir de Jerusalén para comenzar una nueva vida juntos. Jesús le anuncia al pueblo la voluntad de Dios para quienes creen en él.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: Los discípulos de Jesús se desconciertan tras escucharlo decir que entre ellos hay un demonio. Pilatos ordena que maten a todos los judíos para apropiarse del korbán.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Helena se siente abrumada, pero firme en no querer ser monja. Finalmente, Diego reacciona. Isaura se niega a entregarse a Leoncio y éste la castiga en los hierros, pero Malvina interrumpe.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Tomasia comparte cómo fue su vida con el Conde hasta su muerte. Malvina le indica a Isaura que Leoncio no permitirá que Álvaro la compre. Henrique sorprende a todos al decir que entrará a la subasta por Isaura.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Blanca trata de intrigar contra Isaura y Geraldo lo impide. Los postores prohibidos se las arreglan para poder subastar. Henrique rompe nexos con su familia al sentirse traicionado por ellos. Finalmente, la subasta es cancelada.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Álvaro e Isaura tienen un encuentro tierno. El Sargento sigue coqueteando con Tomasia. Leoncio quiere casar a Isaura con Belchior. Blanca insta a Leoncio a que mande a Isaura al tronco.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Blanca se burla de Isaura. André va al quilombo y lleva a Rosa. Muleka siente celos de Rosa, creando una situación complicada con André.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

A former chef fills us in. Finally, a seamstress brings customers to tears with handmade teddy bears dedicated to lost loved ones.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Whether you’re dining out, dressing up, or cheering on the home team, you can go super high-end OR wicked wallet-friendly. Tonight we do both! We try the delicious fare at a local high school restaurant…before hitting a nine-course menu at Somerville’s Tasting Counter. We check out a new luxury option for watching a game inside Boston Garden, while across the street at The Harp find affordable draft beer and plenty of TVs. And if you’re a shoe lover? We meet a gifted local artisan who can make you a pricey custom pair… and a former professional wrestler-turned-cobbler who instead can save your sole.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Dining out, dressing up, or planning a wedding, you can go high-end OR wallet-friendly. Tonight, we do both. We sample the innovative fare at high-end JiangNan Boston…then check out the daily specials at a local spot, Mandarin Cuisine.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As the natural world blooms around us, we dig into our archive to showcase unforgettable springtime stories captured by longtime Chronicle videographer/producer Art Donahue. We meet experts in birdwatching and birdsong, and follow a still photographer who captures early spring beauty – just steps from the Mass Pike. Apple blossoms are a sight to behold at Brookfield Orchards in North Brookfield, while a family farm is an unlikely Shangri-La in Franklin. We also take a trip down south - driving through Virginia’s Shenandoah Valley.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Clean, categorize, purge… scrub, sort, and sage! Tonight we meet people who were built for spring cleaning.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Scanned the real estate listings lately? Inventory is low, prices are sky-high, and some buyers are going to extreme lengths to lock in a purchase. Tonight Shayna Seymour surveys the market, touring homes at the median price in Greater Boston (hint: it’s high!). We meet a Worcester mom who was forced to widen her search to find an affordable home, and families choosing to remodel rather than compete with other buyers. A local home inspector shares a few hints (and horror stories). Finally, if you’re just dreaming, Shayna gets a peek inside a $7 million mansion that offers a taste of the celebrity lifestyle.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: The Benefits Of Dental Implants.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Sick of the snow? We have the perfect solution. Tonight, join Anthony Everett as he heads south to explore the clear waters and stunning beaches on St. John in the U.S. Virgin Islands.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour ventures to Stonington, Connecticut, voted one of the prettiest coastal towns in New England – taking in the salt air on a harbor cruise and hitting a local winery. Ted Reinstein takes a drive to East Greenwich, Rhode Island, where his adventures take him from the town’s historic Main Street to a sailing adventure on Greenwich Bay. And Anthony Everett heads north, visiting South Hero, Vermont, where he checks out a dairy farm that has added wine to its repertoire and bikes to an eatery popular with local foodies.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein meets local growers who are big on blooms. Their peonies and poppies, sweet peas and snapdragons, lift the mood and beautify the landscape. Among the growers: a physician with a side gig as a lavender farmer, a prominent architect who invites Ted into his secret garden on Beacon Hill, and a small-scale flower grower committed to sustainable practices.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re back with another round of stories suggested by you – our Chronicle viewers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You call us, you email us, you load up our social media pages. So tonight we’re making YOU our associate producers with a collection of stories suggested by viewers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From the Boston Tea Party – where tea became a symbol of defiance and independence – to elegant afternoon teas. Tonight we are steeped in tea stories!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re back with another round of stories suggested by you, our viewers!

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour heads north to the charming town of Stowe, nestled at the foot of Mount Mansfield, where she explores bucolic farms, a network of mountain biking trails, and shares a brew on the Vermont Beer Trail (with a member of the Von Trapp family). Shayna meets a local family that has transformed a former ski dorm into a cafe and antiques shop. At Percy Farm, a farming family is dedicated to rebuilding after a devastating fire. And is there a better way to spend a few hours than frolicking with golden retrievers? In Stowe, you can do just that.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: Who knew you could explore endless outdoor adventure, a vibrant food and wine scene, crystal blue freshwater, world-class golf, and a charming downtown- all in Northern Michigan? Join host Hannah Cumler as she showcases the best of Traverse City!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Creative ideas for stress relief during trying times.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: No matter what’s causing your stress, tonight we have ideas for how to reduce it – from diet to exercise to a simple walk in the woods. We talk to a physician about the physical causes of stress – and the physical impact it can have on our health.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Does your closet need a refresher? Spruce up your wardrobe with streetwear: Syn-di-cate is bringing its sleek capsule collection to an international audience. Cambridge boutique Looks specializes in comfy trends for women looking to make an elegant style statement. Brides-to-be seek out a wedding dress “whisperer” at Your Dream Bridal in Sudbury. And who says style is just for humans? Dog parents need look no further than Pawsitivity in Chelmsford for locally-made pooch apparel and accessories…fur real.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Looking for summer fun? If you’re all about the outdoors, you can try electric hydro-foiling; tonight see how Erika Tarantal fared on her maiden voyage. For some, summer is best spent with a good book.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Love true crime? Erika Tarantal talks to the author of Helltown, the story of a serial killer that captivated Cape Cod in the turbulent 1960s. If summer travel is more your style, Erika joins a local travel writer to hit up favorite spots from a new guide to visiting the Cape. Closer to Boston, we meet the owner of a book shop who began selling books from a pushcart before opening her store. Finally, we meet an author whose historical novel highlights immigrant stories from the town of Milford.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Experience the summer scene in Boston from parks, restaurants and outdoor workout classes, find new acitivies in Boston.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Boston culinary icon Lydia Shire is still cooking, still creating – and still inspiring! Tonight she takes us from Chinatown to Cambridge, exploring the dishes and traditions that fuel her imagination—from roast duck to homemade lemongrass oil.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: With three Grammy awards to her name and millions of records sold with The Pointer Sisters, what is Ruth Pointer doing living in a tiny town in central Mass.? Tonight we visit this super septuagenarian at her home (yes, she’s still performing), and meet others who are also shining bright in their golden years. Nicole Estaphan meets the Pom-tastics cheerleading squad, who practice at a local senior center.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Portuguese pastries in Fall River, a French patisserie in Medford, Polish paczki in Salem – all on the menu tonight as we search for international specialties. We meet the Thai-American co-owner of a tiny Somerville shop where a group of friends got together to start a bakery, and on Newbury Street we sample the signature cream puffs at Japanese chain, Beard Papa’s.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If you’re craving gingerbread, Christmas cookies, chocolate babka, or even gluten-free holiday treats, tonight we have you covered.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: There’s just something about the holiday season that kicks the sweet tooth into overdrive. We stop by family-owned shops Hilliard’s in North Easton to spin up homemade candy canes and Winfrey’s on the North Shore where fudge is the main event. At other sweet stops we dig into gooey cinnamon rolls, ghost pepper caramels, peppermint bark (both Christmas and Hanukkah varieties) and get in on the “cocoa bomb” craze. And for a break from the sweet routine we sample specialty roasted nuts at Cacao in Newton.

programme.desc: CityLine5 is WCVB's award-winning urban news and feature magazine talk show.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If you’re craving gingerbread, Christmas cookies, chocolate babka, or even gluten-free holiday treats, tonight we have you covered.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we continue our week of “all things cool.” If public pools and crowded ocean beaches aren’t your perfect way to cool off, here’s another suggestion: swimming holes! Tonight, Anthony Everett heads north in search of nature’s secret streams and (semi) private pools.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Think lobsters are pricey? They’ve got nothing on “glass eels,” now a beyond-booming industry on the coast of Maine. We visit Ellsworth, where at today’s price of $2,200 a pound, there’s heavy competition to net the tiny, wriggling baby eels for export to Asia. We meet some of the lucky few licensed in the glass eel trade, including the so-called “godfather” of the industry, as well as ordinary Mainers whose fortunes have changed thanks to a coveted license. And we talk to Maine law enforcement about the early days of the eel trade, a wild-west scene replete with guns, cash – and eels.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Those topics that just might be off the table in your house, or the ones that make you turn just a little bit red. Tonight, Chronicle is pulling back the curtain on some “taboo topics” - from politics at the dinner table to trips to the restroom.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: The Benefits Of Dental Implants.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: New Englanders love spending time by the water, even in winter. Anthony Everett visits Chatham, on Cape Cod, where he meets a business exec turned fine art photographer, chronicles the rebirth of a local movie theater, and grabs a bite at…the airport? Shayna Seymour visits coastal Freeport, Maine – during a snowstorm!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: They call it the “Bidd-aissance” – the rebirth underway in Biddeford, Maine. Tonight Anthony Everett heads to the former mill town, now becoming a foodie mecca. Finally, Shayna Seymour is exploring Westerly, R.I., where she gets spooked on a ghost tour, shops for books, and overnights at the historic Weekapaug Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Shayna Seymour heads to Lake Sunapee in New Hampshire, where the ice is out, a local farm is bursting with life, and the community is gearing up for peak tourist season.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan heads north to explore lake life in Naples, Maine, where she hops aboard a paddlewheeler on Long Lake; stumbles across Shaquille O’Neal’s sneaker at a unique shop; and bites into a “Sebagel” at a local bagel shop

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: They call it the “Bidd-aissance” – the rebirth underway in Biddeford, Maine. Tonight Anthony Everett heads to the former mill town, now fast becoming a foodie mecca. He samples local seafood; visits historic City Theater; and checks out the city’s hip new hotel. Erika Tarantal heads south to Marion, Mass., stopping in its quaint downtown; exploring the local natural history museum and a historic public golf course; and sampling the local catch. Finally, Shayna Seymour is exploring Westerly, R.I., where she gets spooked on a ghost tour, shops for books, and overnights at the historic Weekapaug Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett heads to midcoast Maine, where in Rockport he discovers a tiny bookshop with an inspiring origin story; encounters some special cows at a working farm/nature preserve; and takes in the ocean views from the classic Samoset Resort.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett uses a tank and a ferry to get to this summer destination – Block Island. He gets a taste of summer life on the Block, hits up local restaurant The Oar and the revamped Block Island Beach House, and meets the gifted artist behind the charming “glass float” project. Meanwhile, Shayna Seymour pays a summer visit to ever-charming Mystic, Conn. She frolics with the sea life at Mystic Aquarium, travels back in time at Mystic Seaport Museum, makes a quick stop at Mystic Pizza (naturally) and checks out the charming Whalers Inn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Erika Tarantal hits Providence, R.I., where she checks out the art scene, tours a historic hotel, and even takes a blacksmithing class. While Shayna Seymour visits Portsmouth, N.H and Nicole Estaphan goes to Portland, Maine.

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: There's nothing like Benzie County! Join host Hannah Cumler as she celebrates the powerful women behind a thriving family resort, the Michiganders running the Midwest’s first grain-to-glass distillery, and toasts this can't-miss destination.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we visit coastal communities that are waking up from their winter slumber. Shayna Seymour heads north to Kennebunkport, Maine, where she samples ice cream with outrageous flavors at Rococo Dessert Bar, visits an artisan boutique, and beds down at the historic Kennebunkport Inn.

programme.desc: Three indigenous makers set out to create a traditional gorget representing their ancestry and Oklahoma City. Tensions rise when an unanticipated addition is brought to the challenge forcing on-the-fly changes. Whose design will soar above the rest?

programme.desc: Esquire’s Dave Holmes chats with Drag Race’s Peppermint and shares an Esquire-approved itinerary for your next trip to Sacramento.

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Chefs Jessica Peconi and Dee Dixon are getting saucy in today’s cooking challenge. Can the chefs craft the most savory dips, dressings, or relish? Or will they get Lost in the Sauce?

programme.desc: Peter’s first love may be Taylor Swift, but he’s ready to let another person in. Who will sweep this Switfy off of his feet and into his very own fairytale?
